steps.title: Isolate $j$
steps.content: Start with $$7j = k + 12m.$$ Since $j$ is multiplied by $7$, divide both sides by $7$ to solve for $j$.

steps.title: Divide both sides by 7
steps.content: $$j = \frac{k + 12m}{7}.$$ The entire right side, including both $k$ and $12m$, is divided by $7$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $j=\frac{k}{7}+12m$ $j=\frac{k}{7}+12m$ — divides only $k$ by $7$, leaving $12m$ undivided. Incorrect.
  • $j=k+\frac{12m}{7}$ $j=k+\frac{12m}{7}$ — divides only $12m$ by $7$, leaving $k$ undivided. Incorrect.
  • $j=7(k+12m)$ $j=7(k+12m)$ — multiplies by $7$ instead of dividing. Incorrect.
  • $j=\frac{k+12m}{7}$ $j=\frac{k+12m}{7}$ — correctly divides the whole right side by $7$. Correct.
Choice D is the correct answer.
